title: GC floor must retroactively clear applied events <= floor for order-independent convergence; add regression test around NamespaceGcMarker apply (see gc_floor_machine)

acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] No ad-hoc `unsafe` blocks remain in `beads-surface/src/ipc/client.rs` for autostart FD cleanup.
- [ ] Existing autostart/lifecycle integration tests remain green and non-flaky.
- [ ] A regression test or stress harness demonstrates no inherited-FD hang in the covered scenario.
- [ ] `cargo clippy --all-features -- -D warnings` passes.
- [ ] `cargo test --all-features` passes.
description:
**Problem**
`beads-surface` IPC client autostart currently uses `unsafe` (`CommandExt::pre_exec` + raw fd close loop) to prevent file-descriptor leakage into daemon children. This fixed a real test-hang/flakiness class, but leaves maintainability/policy drift in a boundary crate.

Current path:
- `crates/beads-surface/src/ipc/client.rs`
  - `prepare_daemon_spawn_command`
  - `close_non_stdio_fds`

**Goal**
Preserve the FD-leak fix behavior while removing local `unsafe` from this code path (or strictly isolating it behind a dedicated, justified boundary).

**Files**
- crates/beads-surface/src/ipc/client.rs
- crates/beads-daemon/** (if daemon-side early-close strategy is used)
- crates/beads-rs/tests/integration/** (behavior/flakiness regression coverage)
design:
1. Evaluate safe alternatives to current `pre_exec` strategy (preferred: daemon-side early descriptor cleanup before runtime setup).
2. Keep behavior invariant: daemon spawn must not inherit arbitrary test harness/capture pipe FDs.
3. If daemon-side move is used, ensure cleanup runs before any long-lived background activity.
4. Add regression coverage that would catch descriptor-leak hangs in lifecycle/concurrency tests.
5. Document rationale and final boundary ownership in architecture notes.
title: Remove unsafe from IPC autostart FD cleanup path without regressing flake fix

description:
**Problem**
Realtime integration tests start the daemon via `bd` auto-start but do not shut it down. `RealtimeFixture` drops only restore env (no `Request::Shutdown`), and `AdminFixture` has no Drop cleanup. `tests/critical_path.rs` and `tests/migration.rs` also auto-start the daemon and never shut it down (only one test does best-effort). This leaves orphaned `bd daemon run` processes, stale sockets/locks, and makes repeated test runs flaky on developer machines and CI.

Evidence:
- `tests/fixtures/realtime.rs` Drop only restores env (lines 77-80).
- `tests/phase7_admin.rs`, `tests/phase7_admin_status.rs`, `tests/phase7_subscribe.rs` use `RealtimeFixture::start_daemon()` and never stop it.
- `tests/critical_path.rs`/`tests/migration.rs` use `bd` CLI without teardown.

**Design**
- Add a shared test helper `shutdown_daemon(runtime_dir: &Path)` (new `tests/fixtures/daemon_runtime.rs` or similar).
- Implement shutdown by sending IPC `Request::Shutdown` to `beads/daemon.sock`, waiting for ack or socket removal; fall back to SIGTERM/SIGKILL if the process is still alive after a timeout.
- Make the helper idempotent (missing socket/meta -> Ok).
- Call this helper from Drop for `RealtimeFixture` and `AdminFixture`, and from the runtime fixture used by `critical_path`/`migration`.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] Any test that auto-starts the daemon shuts it down during Drop (socket + meta removed).
- [ ] `cargo test` leaves no `bd daemon run` processes behind.
- [ ] Helper is used by phase7 realtime tests and other integration tests that spawn the daemon.

**Files:**
- tests/fixtures/realtime.rs
- tests/phase7_admin.rs
- tests/phase7_admin_status.rs
- tests/phase7_subscribe.rs
- tests/critical_path.rs
- tests/migration.rs
- tests/fixtures/daemon_runtime.rs (new)
title: tests: realtime fixtures leak daemon processes

description:
**Problem**
`crates/beads-rs/tests/integration/repl/**` exercises daemon/daemon-core protocol state machines, ACK/WANT semantics, backpressure, and WAL range reads. The suite relies on lower-level repl fixtures under `crates/beads-rs/tests/integration/fixtures/{repl_frames,repl_peer,repl_transport}.rs` plus helper slices from `identity`, `store_dir`, and `wal`. These are daemon-owned concerns, not assembly behavior.

**Design**
- Move the repl protocol suite to daemon-owned tests, most likely `crates/beads-daemon/tests/**` with support modules. Use `beads-daemon-core` only if a subset is purely frame/proto owned after inspection.
- Move or rewrite only the fixture helpers the repl suite actually needs.
- Remove the moved repl module declarations from `crates/beads-rs/tests/integration.rs`.

**Files To Study**
- `crates/beads-rs/tests/integration/repl/**`
- `crates/beads-rs/tests/integration/fixtures/{repl_frames,repl_peer,repl_transport,identity,store_dir,wal}.rs`
- `crates/beads-daemon/src/testkit/mod.rs`

**Acceptance**
- [ ] Repl protocol tests no longer live under `crates/beads-rs/tests/integration/repl/**`
- [ ] Repl-support fixtures move into daemon-owned test support
- [ ] relevant daemon test target passes
- [ ] `cargo test -p beads-rs --test integration` still passes after the repl modules leave

notes.author: darin@book
notes.content:
Audit:
- workspace: /Users/darin/src/personal/beads-rs
- jj: bbe3c7c2 beads-rs-gou3.7.3.5: move repl suite into daemon tests
- moved repl integration suite from crates/beads-rs/tests/integration/repl/** to crates/beads-daemon/tests/repl/**
- moved repl support fixtures from crates/beads-rs/tests/integration/fixtures/{repl_frames,repl_peer,repl_transport}.rs to crates/beads-daemon/tests/support/**
- rewired moved tests to use crate::support::* and beads_daemon::paths instead of beads-rs assembly helpers
- removed repl module registration from crates/beads-rs/tests/integration.rs and dropped the stale daemon_boundary::repl re-export
- verification:
  - cargo test -p beads-daemon --features test-harness --test repl
  - cargo test -p beads-rs --test integration
  - cargo check -p beads-rs --all-features
  - cargo fmt --all --check

title: Move repl protocol integration suite into daemon-owned tests
